summary: Iteration 253 — Final pre-deploy regression for Avatar.tsx blurhash fix. Verified: placeholder={null} added to OptimizedImage in Avatar.tsx correctly prevents blurhash length mismatch crash. All 5 tabs (Home, Contacts, Activity, Inbox, Hub) load without 'Something went wrong' ErrorBoundary. Contact list renders Avatar initials/photos with no crash. Contact detail page navigates correctly. Matt login goes to /home not /onboarding. Backend: 21/21 tests pass (rerun of iteration_252 suite).
critical_code_review_comments: Avatar.tsx line 141: placeholder={null} correctly added — fix verified working. No blurhash crash on any tab.
One 404 error logged in console (likely a missing image resource) — not related to Avatar fix, observed since iteration_252

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 tests passed — same suite as iteration_252)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (Admin login→/home, Contacts tab no crash + Avatar initials visible, Activity tab no crash + Avatar visible, Inbox no crash, Hub no crash, Contact detail loads, Matt login→/home not /onboarding, Matt Contacts+Activity no crash)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The blurhash crash is fully fixed. Avatar.tsx now uses placeholder={null} on OptimizedImage preventing expo-image from attempting to decode a PLACEHOLDER_BLUR hash at small avatar dimensions. All 5 tabs load without crash for both admin and regular user. 21/21 backend tests pass. App is safe to deploy.
